summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their organizational and regular meeting for January 2025. The board delivered a comprehensive state of the township address highlighting 2024 accomplishments including nearly $7 million in secured funding, Fire Station 18 reconstruction, major paving projects, and infrastructure improvements. Trustee Lisa Allen was elected as the 2025 board chairperson and Trustee Bob Hauserman as vice chair. The board also addressed organizational items for 2025, accepted personnel changes, and heard a resident proposal to explore an electrical aggregation program for cost savings.
decisions: Adopted the agenda for the meeting
Approved payment of bills for December 17, 2024 through January 14, 2025
Nominated and elected Trustee Lisa Allen as 2025 Board of Trustees chairperson
Nominated and elected Trustee Bob Hauserman as 2025 vice chair
Adopted the 2025 board of trustees regular meeting schedule (second and fourth Tuesdays at 6 PM)
Set 2025 standard mileage rate at 70 cents per business mile per IRS guidelines
Approved Resolution R-001-2025 authorizing sale of surplus items via govdeals.com
Set community forum rules with five-minute time limit before agenda items
Directed Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials
Set 2025 zoning commission and board of zoning appeals member compensation at $50 per month, not to exceed $599 annually
Adopted 2025 zoning and service department fee schedules with no changes from 2024
Set trick-or-treat date to be determined by Clermont County Township Association
Scheduled Goshen cleanup day for Saturday, June 7, 2025
Accepted resignation of Officer James Beckelheimer effective January 11, 2025
Approved Officer McAllister to attend subject control instructor training ($1,000 registration)
Approved SROs Alcorn and Groves to attend SRO conference in Sandusky, Ohio July 7-9, 2025 ($750 per officer)
Accepted $2,500 donation from American Legion Post 256 for canine program
Adjourned into executive session at 6:41 PM per ORC 121.22 G1
Accepted donation of six $25 Texas Roadhouse gift cards from Haas Homes and Bailey Families to service department
Approved job description and advertisement for part-time planning and zoning director
Approved hire of part-time firefighter paramedic Nathan at $22.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
Approved hire of part-time firefighter EMT Grant Brown at $17.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
Approved hire of part-time firefighter EMT Will Pisani at $17.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
Adjourned at 7:44 PM
action_items: Administrator Bob Rose to investigate electrical aggregation program, consulting with Loveland and Miami Township administrators
Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials
Service Director Chad Meadows and team to develop plan for cleanup day location for June 7, 2025
Officer McAllister to attend subject control instructor training January 27 - February 14
Advertise for part-time planning and zoning director position
Trustee Hauserman to discuss electrical aggregation process at Thursday Township Association meeting
key_amounts: $67,350.49 estimated cost for two snowstorms (144 overtime hours, 115 straight hours, 420 gallons fuel, 308 tons of salt, 2,045 miles)
70 cents per mile 2025 IRS mileage rate
$50 per month compensation for zoning commission and BZA members, not to exceed $599 annually
$1,000 registration for subject control instructor training
$750 per officer for SRO conference registration
$2,500 donation from American Legion Post 256 for canine program
$150 in Texas Roadhouse gift cards donated to service department (six $25 cards)
$831,940 for paving projects completed fall 2023 (70%+ grant funded)
$1.7 million received from Federal American Rescue Plan (ARPA) in 2022
Nearly $7 million in federal, state, and county funds secured over past two years
$60,000+ saved through renegotiated contracts (healthcare, IT, internet, payroll, unemployment)
$97,000 additional interest revenue from new interest-earning savings account (18,000% increase)
$22.50 per hour rate for part-time firefighter paramedic Nathan
$17.50 per hour rate for part-time firefighter EMTs Grant Brown and Will Pisani
Estimated $29.76 per month savings per household under electrical aggregation proposal
Estimated ~$200,000 per month community-wide savings under electrical aggregation
notable_topics: 2025 organizational meeting: election of chair and vice chair
State of the Township address summarizing 2024 accomplishments
Snow removal operations and costs for two major snowstorms (13-15 inches total)
Resident proposal for electrical aggregation program to reduce electricity costs
Fire Station 18 reconstruction update - foundation work expected first week of March
Clermont County Auditor's office outreach and open data resources
Officer resignation (Beckelheimer to City of Lebanon PD)
New part-time planning and zoning director position creation
American Legion donation to canine program
Community recognition of service department snow removal efforts
Homearama event coming to Goshen Township at Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm
Clermont County Township Association meeting Thursday at Batavia Township
attendees: Greg Gentile (Clermont County Deputy Auditor of Outside Services)
notes: This was a combined organizational and regular meeting. Executive session lasted approximately one hour (6:41 PM to 7:41 PM) to discuss personnel matters. Trustee Hauserman is president of the Clermont County Township Association. The first name of the newly hired firefighter paramedic appears to have been cut off in the transcript (referred to only as 'Nathan'). Goshen Township is noted as the fourth largest community in Clermont County, larger than all cities, and nearly larger than Milford and Loveland combined. The roll call order was changed for 2025, causing brief confusion among trustees.
title: BOT 1-14-25
